1.q: Overexpression of HbNHX2 led to decreased malondialdehyde (MDA) and superoxide anion (O2−) levels, while it enhanced the activities of superoxide dismutase (SOD), peroxidase (POD), and catalase (CAT).

2.q: The biochemical co-immunoprecipitation (co-IP) assay showed that HbCBL1/4/10 proteins were detected using HA antibodies for the affinity-purified protein harboring a Myc-tagged HbCIPK2.

5.q: We further identified the HbCIPK2-interacting proteins including upstream Ca2+ sensors, HbCBL1, HbCBL4, and HbCBL10, and downstream phosphorylated targets, the voltage-gated K+ channel HbVGKC1 and SOS1-like transporter HbSOS1L.

6.q: We further identified the HbCIPK2-interacting proteins including upstream Ca2+ sensors, HbCBL1, HbCBL4, and HbCBL10, and downstream phosphorylated targets, the voltage-gated K+ channel HbVGKC1 and SOS1-like transporter HbSOS1L.

7.q: HbCBL4/10–HbCIPK2 complex modulated HbSOS1L to exclude Na+. This discovery suggested that crosstalk between the sodium response and the potassium uptake signaling pathways indeed exists for HbCIPK2 as the signal hub.

10.q: The in vitro kinase assays using GST–cHbVGKC1 were performed... HbCIPK2-CA (CA, constitutively active)... downstream phosphorylated targets, the voltage-gated K+ channel HbVGKC1.

11.q: The in vitro kinase assays involving His-c1/c2HbSOS1L were performed as described by Quintero et al. (2011)... downstream phosphorylated targets... SOS1-like transporter HbSOS1L.

12.q: HbCBL1 combined with HbCIPK2 could activate HbVGKC1 to absorb K+, while the HbCBL4/10–HbCIPK2 complex modulated HbSOS1L to exclude Na+.

15.q: HbERF6 functioned as a transcription factor which can bind to the GCC-box of the HbCIPK2 promoter to activate its expression.

16.q: CBL and its target protein CBL-interacting protein kinase (CIPK) constitute the CBL-CIPK signal network system... HbCIPK2 contributing to salt tolerance in wild barley.
The calcineurin B-like (CBL)–CBL-interacting protein kinase (CIPK) complex is a critical signaling transducer... a Ca2+ signaling mechanism.

17.q: The two transgenic lines showed better growth under salt conditions than athak5 and wild type, especially under 100 mM NaCl... indicating HbHAK1 can mediate salt tolerance of plants.

18.q: The HbCBL4 and HbCBL10 transgenic lines dramatically rescued the salt-sensitive phenotype of sos3: improved growth, significantly longer roots, and more lateral roots under the 100–175 mM NaCl treatments.

19.q: Overexpression of HbNHX2 led to decreased malondialdehyde (MDA) and superoxide anion (O2−) levels, while it enhanced the activities of superoxide dismutase (SOD), peroxidase (POD), and catalase (CAT).

20.q: HbHAK1 showed high-affinity K+ transporter activity in athak5 to cope with low-K+ or salt stresses... its strong K+ uptake promoted the improvement of salt tolerance.

21.q: the HbCBL4 and HbCBL10 transgenic lines dramatically rescued the salt-sensitive phenotype of sos3.

22.q: The NHX gene family plays a vital role in regulating intracellular Na+/K+ balance, pH homeostasis... HbNHX2 not only contributes to ion and osmotic balance
